assets.name: lecture_07_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 04 is the best of an almost identical set because it gives the most settled, complete view of the board at this subtitle moment: the Lorentz-force equation is fully legible, the large bracket and cross-product term are clear, and the frame is minimally cluttered. The partial sketch at the left edge is preserved as layout evidence without competing with the main formula.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: You can write the equations without the vector potential,
assets.caption_hint: Lorentz force law on the board

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: Candidate 03 is the best balance of completeness and legibility. It preserves the board's stepwise structure from the ordinary-time action to the explicit Lagrangian, which is exactly the setup for the Euler-Lagrange equations, while avoiding the heavier hand-and-paper occlusion that appears in candidate 04. Candidates 01 and 02 show less of the derivation ladder and give a less complete mathematical context.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Euler Lagrange equations for a given Lagrangian.
